seoTitle: How to Change Your Custom Domain Name in Gumroad
metaDescription: Learn how to update your Gumroad custom domain, remove the old one, and configure the CNAME record needed to point it to your storefront.
intro: If you've outgrown your current custom domain or need to point Gumroad to a new web address, updating it involves changes on both Gumroad and your domain registrar. This guide covers removing your old domain, adding the new one, and setting up the DNS record that connects them.

steps.heading: Open advanced settings
steps.body: Go to your Gumroad settings and find the advanced section where custom domain options live.

steps.heading: Update your custom domain field
steps.body: Remove the old domain entry and type in the new domain you want to connect.

steps.heading: Log into your domain registrar
steps.body: Access the DNS management panel for wherever your domain is registered.

steps.heading: Add or update the CNAME record
steps.body: Point your domain's CNAME record to Gumroad's target address so the connection works.

steps.heading: Confirm the connection
steps.body: Check your Gumroad settings for a status indicator confirming the new domain is verified and active.

faq.question: How long does it take for a domain change to work?
faq.answer: DNS changes can take time to propagate, so it may not connect instantly after you update the CNAME record.

faq.question: Do I need to remove the old domain before adding a new one?
faq.answer: Yes, updating the custom domain field typically means replacing the old value with the new one.

faq.question: What DNS record type does Gumroad require?
faq.answer: Gumroad connects custom domains using a CNAME record pointed to their target endpoint.

faq.question: Will my product links break during the domain change?
faq.answer: There may be a brief window during DNS propagation, so it's worth planning the switch during low-traffic periods.
